Post-OpEx, BoJ, Denmark | Intraday post (20/Jan)

Weekly post:

PCE, BoJ rate decision | Weekly post (20-23/Jan)

In this post I cover the AI/tech boom, the restructuring of the geopolitical landscape and its effects on the economy, BoJ, geography and the coming crisis:

Very big spike in volume today. So market is expected to be customer-driven, not MM flow-driven. I don’t think that the rebound is timely yet.

The events have nothing to do with Greenland. If Greenland happens = 20-30% blackswan. I covered it in the weekly post, that the short-term main concern is Japan. Plus, now Danish pension fund sold like $100 million worth US treassuries, that is a relatively small amount, but it reinforces the global sentiment shift.

Let’s look at the daily positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

The speed profile is net flat-to-negative, customers are buying volatility into both direction, but slightly more to the upside.

Centroid is at 6812.28

Downside pivot is at 6819.16with target of 6738.15The pivot and centroid area is sticky into the PM session, if 6790 can be held. So is the downside pivot, with 6768/70 as “border”. The downside target is pretty supportive but not “reversionish”. Reversion is either happens from 6780/90 or nowhere.

Upside pivot is at 6839.24with target of 6904.67The upside target is also sticky into PM, is spot is above 6890, but the upside path is full of resistances. Main resistance is at 6865. Here the reversion has the highest probability, back towards the centroid.Or if it breaks during the first two hours, it can pin to 6875, or go up to the target, if 6890 is broken. But it should be broken during the AM session (higher probability).

OptionsDepth Heatmap
